page: Amazon Web Services c9gd.medium is a compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AWS Graviton processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, this server features 1 physical core and 1 vCPU running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation and minimal virtualization overhead.

The instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio typical of compute-optimized configurations. For local storage, the c9gd.medium includes a bundled 59 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, providing high-speed local disk access directly attached to the host. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

From a resource density perspective, the c9gd.medium offers a cost-efficient entry point for arm64-based compute workloads that require local NVMe storage. However, the single-core configuration and 2.0 GB memory limit its suitability for multi-threaded or memory-intensive applications. This instance is best suited for lightweight compute tasks, microservices, single-threaded application testing, and edge web serving where local scratch space is required.
description: Amazon Web Services c9gd.medium is a compute-optimized, arm64-based instance powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton processor. Running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this single-core instance features 1 vCPU, 1 physical core, and 2.0 GB of system memory. It includes a bundled 59 GB NVMe SSD local instance store volume, providing high-speed, direct-attached storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The server offers a qualitative balance of compute performance and local storage efficiency for arm64 architectures, though its single-core design and 2.0 GB RAM capacity present tradeoffs for memory-heavy or highly parallelized applications. The c9gd.medium is suited for lightweight compute workloads, microservices, single-threaded application testing, and web serving tasks requiring fast local scratch space.

tagline: A single-core arm64 compute instance featuring dedicated Graviton processing and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Single-core AWS Graviton processor running at 2.8 GHz on arm64 architecture
Dedicated CPU allocation with 1 vCPU and 2.0 GB of system memory
Bundled 59 GB NVMe SSD local instance store volume for fast scratch space
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Suited for lightweight microservices, single-threaded testing, and edge web serving
